records.ety: [L. epigramma, fr. Gr. <?/ inscription, epigram, fr. <?/ to write upon, 'epi` upon + <?/ to write: cf. F. pigramme. See Graphic.]

defs: A short poem treating concisely and pointedly of a single thought or event. The modern epigram is so contrived as to surprise the reader with a witticism or ingenious turn of thought, and is often satirical in character.
quotes: Dost thou think I care for a satire or an epigram?
notes: ☞ Epigrams were originally inscription on tombs, statues, temples, triumphal arches, etc.

defs: An effusion of wit; a bright thought tersely and sharply expressed, whether in verse or prose.

quotes: Antithesis, i. e., bilateral stroke, is the soul of epigram in its later and technical signification.
